top_evidence.title: A remarkable finale - but will 13 chaotic minutes cost Man City title?
top_evidence.summary: A mad-cap 13 minutes from Manchester City in the second half of their six-goal thriller at Everton could prove to be their undoing in this season's Premier League title race.
top_evidence.author: Shamoon Hafez - Manchester City reporter at Hill Dickinson Stadium

top_evidence.title: Arsenal control their Premier League title fate
top_evidence.summary: Arsenal’s simple task just became even simpler: win three matches, win the Premier League. With Manchester City drawing 3-3 at Everton, Arsenal control their Premier League destiny. Pep Guardiola’s side needed to win out to force Arsenal to do the same to finish level on points. They failed. The Gunners no longer need to worry […]
top_evidence.author: Aaron Lerner

top_evidence.title: Everton 3-3 Man City: What Guardiola and Doku said
top_evidence.summary: The British Premier League - we know that. Jeremy Doku's equaliser (96:49) was Manchester City's third-latest goal on record (since 2006-07) in a Premier League game after John Stones' strike against Arsenal in September 2024 (97:14) and Gabriel Jesus' goal against Everton in February 2019 (96:52).
top_evidence.author: BBC
